dublinCore.dc:title: 119 HR 8104 IH: Fertilizer Transparency Act of 2026
dublinCore.dc:rights: Pursuant to Title 17 Section 105 of the United States Code, this file is not subject to copyright protection and is in the public domain.

action.action-desc: Mr. Johnson of South Dakota (for himself, Ms. Craig, Mr. Finstad, Ms. Davids of Kansas, Mrs. Miller-Meeks, Ms. Budzinski, Mrs. Hinson, Mr. Riley of New York, Mr. Feenstra, Mr. Panetta, Mr. Nunn of Iowa, and Mr. Sorensen) introduced the following bill; which was referred to the Committee on Agriculture

form.official-title: To amend the Agricultural Marketing Act of 1946 to establish a mandatory price reporting program for fertilizer, and for other purposes.

section.text: This Act may be cited as the Fertilizer Transparency Act of 2026.
Subtitle A of the Agricultural Marketing Act of 1946 (7 U.S.C. 1621 et seq.) is amended by adding at the end the following:

paragraph.text: The term affiliate means, with respect to a manufacturer or wholesaler (excluding a cooperative), a person that directly or indirectly owns, controls, or holds, with voting power, not less than 5 percent of the outstanding voting securities of the manufacturer or wholesaler (excluding a cooperative).

subparagraph.text: The term cooperative means—

clause.text: an association of agricultural producers acting pursuant to—

subclause.text: the Act entitled An Act to authorize association of producers of agricultural products (commonly known as the Capper-Volstead Act) (7 U.S.C. 291 et seq.);
the Agricultural Adjustment Act (7 U.S.C. 601 et seq.), reenacted with amendments by the Agricultural Marketing Agreement Act of 1937; or
the Act of July 2, 1926 (commonly known as the Cooperative Marketing Act) (7 U.S.C. 451 et seq.);

clause.text: a farmers’ cooperative organization described in subsection (b)(1) of section 521 of the Internal Revenue Code of 1986 and exempt from taxation under subsection (a) of that section; and
an association of agricultural producers otherwise operating on a cooperative basis for the benefit of its members.

subparagraph.text: The term cooperative includes any entity not less than 25 percent of which is owned by a cooperative, as defined in subparagraph (A).

paragraph.text: The term marketed means the sale or other disposition in commerce of—

subparagraph.text: nitrogen, phosphorous, or potassium for use as fertilizer; or
a fertilizer product.

paragraph.text: The term retailer means a person or entity that primarily sells fertilizer products at retail.
The term Secretary means the Secretary of Agriculture.
The term wholesaler means any person or entity, not including a cooperative, engaged in the business of buying and selling fertilizer or fertilizer products for resale or distribution.

subsection.text: The Secretary shall establish a program of fertilizer and fertilizer product price information reporting that will—

paragraph.text: provide timely, accurate, and reliable market information that can be readily understood by farmers and market participants;
facilitate more informed marketing decisions; and
promote competition in the fertilizer and fertilizer products industry.

subsection.header: General reporting provisions applicable to manufacturers, wholesalers, and the Secretary
subsection.text: Whenever the prices or quantities of fertilizer or fertilizer products are required to be reported or published under this section, the prices or quantities shall be categorized so as to clearly delineate—

paragraph.text: the prices or quantities, as applicable, of the fertilizer or fertilizer product marketed in the United States by a domestic manufacturer or wholesaler or an affiliate of a domestic manufacturer or wholesaler; and

the prices or quantities, as applicable, of the fertilizer or fertilizer product marketed in the United States by a foreign manufacturer or wholesaler or an affiliate of a foreign manufacturer or wholesaler.

The corporate officers or officially designated representatives of each manufacturer or wholesaler of nitrogen, phosphorous, or potassium for use as fertilizer shall report to the Secretary at least weekly—

subparagraph.text: the prices, as marketed, for nitrogen, phosphorous, or potassium, as applicable; and
the quantities of nitrogen, phosphorous, or potassium, as applicable, manufactured and marketed, as applicable.

paragraph.text: The corporate officers or officially designated representatives of each manufacturer or wholesaler of a fertilizer product shall report to the Secretary at least weekly—

subparagraph.text: the prices for the fertilizer product; and
the quantity of the fertilizer product manufactured or marketed, as applicable.

paragraph.header: Mandatory reporting exemption for cooperatives and non-manufacturer retailers; voluntary reporting
paragraph.text: The Secretary shall—

subparagraph.text: exempt all cooperatives and retailers (except for retailers that are also manufacturers) from any mandatory price reporting under this section; and
provide a mechanism for cooperatives and retailers to voluntarily and confidentially report the prices and quantities described in subparagraphs (A) and (B) of paragraphs (1) and (2)—

clause.text: through the program established under subsection (e).

paragraph.text: The Secretary shall make the information reported to the Secretary under this subsection available to the public—

subparagraph.text: not less frequently than weekly; and
in a manner that ensures the information is published—

clause.text: on a regional or statewide basis, as the Secretary determines to be appropriate.

paragraph.text: The Secretary may conduct a competitive effects analysis of the information reported to the Secretary under this subsection.
The Secretary shall establish a program within Market News of the Agricultural Marketing Service—

subparagraph.text: to conduct not less frequently than weekly surveys of retail fertilizer prices;
to obtain commercially available estimates of the retail prices described in subparagraph (A); and
to provide State or regional estimates or benchmarks and formulas to allow estimation of local prices.

paragraph.text: The program established under paragraph (1) shall supplement, and not supplant, existing input price collection activities of the Secretary.

The Secretary shall, directly or through 1 or more cooperative agreements with 1 or more affiliated agricultural research programs, not less frequently than weekly summarize and make available on a dashboard or other resource easily accessible to farmers and market participants—

subparagraph.text: the information reported to the Secretary under subsection (d); and
the retail survey prices and commercially available estimates obtained under subsection (e).

paragraph.text: In carrying out paragraph (1), the Secretary shall aggregate the information and data in a manner that prevents confidential business information and the identity of persons, including parties to a contract, from being disclosed publicly.

subparagraph.text: Subject to subparagraph (B), no officer, employee, or agent of the United States shall, without the consent of the manufacturer, wholesaler, or other person concerned, divulge or make known in any manner any facts or information regarding the business of the manufacturer, wholesaler, or other person that was acquired through reporting required under subsection (d).
Information obtained by the Secretary under subsection (d) may be disclosed—

clause.text: to agents or employees of the Department of Agriculture in the course of their official duties under this subtitle;
as directed by the Secretary or the Attorney General, for enforcement purposes; or
by a court of competent jurisdiction.

subparagraph.header: Disclosure under freedom of information act
subparagraph.text: Notwithstanding any other provision of law, no facts or information obtained under this subtitle shall be disclosed in accordance with section 552 of title 5, United States Code.

subsection.text: Not less frequently than once every 2 years, the Secretary shall review the information required to be reported to the Secretary by manufacturers and wholesalers under this section.

If the Secretary determines under a review under subsection (g) that information required to be reported under this section no longer accurately reflects the methods by which nitrogen, phosphorous, and potassium for use as fertilizer or fertilizer products are valued and priced by manufacturers and wholesalers, the Secretary shall, after public notice and an opportunity for comment, promulgate regulations to specify additional information that shall be reported under this section.

paragraph.text: Nothing in this section modifies, impairs, or supersedes the operation of any of the antitrust laws.
In this subsection, the term antitrust laws—

subparagraph.text: has the meaning given the term in subsection (a) of the first section of the Clayton Act (15 U.S.C. 12); and
includes section 5 of the Federal Trade Commission Act (15 U.S.C. 45) to the extent that such section applies to unfair methods of competition.
